name: Bethany Chen
technical_background: No formal coding experience. Strong digital literacy from 8 years integrating educational technology (LMS, simulations, assessment platforms). Master's in Education with focus on curriculum design and differentiated instruction. Comfortable with Google Workspace, learning analytics dashboards, and basic data interpretation.

current_profile.summary: High school STEM teacher with 8 years of classroom experience and a Master's in Education. Designs curriculum for AP Biology and Chemistry, runs teacher training workshops, and has been a department technology lead for 3 years. Integrates digital tools including Google Classroom, Desmos, PhET simulations, and various assessment platforms. Has experimented extensively with ChatGPT for lesson plan drafts, differentiation ideas, and creating practice problems. Uses Khan Academy AI tutor with students and has explored Quizlet AI for adaptive flashcard generation. Comfortable creating rubrics, analyzing student performance data, and presenting to faculty and parents. No coding background but strong analytical thinking from STEM teaching. Has not built AI systems or worked with APIs.
technical_skills: Educational data analysis (grade distributions, growth metrics)
soft_skills: Curriculum development and alignment to standards
current_profile.ai_experience: Regular user of ChatGPT for lesson plan drafts, differentiation strategies, and creating practice problems at multiple difficulty levels. Has explored Khan Academy AI tutor with students and Quizlet AI for adaptive flashcard generation. Comfortable writing basic prompts for educational content but approaches are ad hoc rather than systematic. Has attended two district-sponsored AI in education workshops. Has not built AI systems, worked with APIs, or designed AI-powered learning tools — strictly an end user.

learning_intent: Wants to design AI-enhanced learning experiences at scale, moving from classroom teacher to instructional designer at an EdTech company. Motivated by the personalization potential of AI for equitable learning outcomes. Goal: become an AI-literate instructional designer who can prototype prompt-based tutoring systems, design evaluation criteria for AI-generated educational content, collaborate with product and engineering teams, and ensure responsible AI use in student-facing applications.

target_jd.title: AI Learning Experience Designer / Instructional Designer (AI)
requirements: Design AI-enhanced learning journeys, lesson plans, and adaptive assessments
Prototype prompts and content templates for AI tutors, study assistants, and feedback generators
Use learning analytics to iterate on content effectiveness and personalization strategies
Ensure responsible AI use: student privacy, age-appropriate content, bias mitigation, and transparency
Collaborate with engineering and product teams to integrate AI into learning platforms
Design evaluation rubrics and quality criteria for AI-generated educational content
Create prompt libraries and style guides for consistent AI-assisted content generation
Develop teacher and student onboarding materials for AI-powered learning tools
Conduct user research with students and educators to inform AI feature design
Monitor and improve AI tutor interactions using conversation analysis and outcome data

expected_skill_gaps.description: Understand LLM basics, limitations (hallucinations in educational content), multimodal capabilities, and privacy/bias fundamentals with a student-safety lens.
Systematic prompt patterns for tutoring/coaching: few-shot examples, rubrics as prompts, iteration loops, and structured outputs for assessment generation.
Define quality metrics for tutoring output, create golden sets for lesson outcomes, and understand basic evaluation types for educational AI.
Learning design with AI: integrating AI into pedagogy, classroom workflows, and adaptive learning systems.
Explain AI behaviors and limitations to educators, parents, and administrators. Write clear AI usage guidelines for school contexts.
Student data safety: understanding AI security threats, data leakage risks, and safe content generation for minors.
AI governance basics for education: privacy regulations (FERPA, COPPA), responsible AI guidelines, and content generation ethics.
Building systematic personal AI workflows and teaching others AI skills — critical for training fellow educators.
